question_text: A green object is illuminated with red light. Describe what will happen and explain why.
correct_answer: The object will appear black (or very dark) because green objects absorb red light and reflect green light. Since no green light is available, there is no light to reflect.
explanation: Objects only reflect certain colours and absorb others. A green object can only appear green if green light is available to reflect. Red light is absorbed, not reflected.
